question: What integer-rounded percentage of the total length of the harlequin shrimp recorded in Omar Valencfia-Mendez 2017 paper was the sea star fed to the same type of shrimp in G. Curt Fiedler's 2002 paper?

research_queries: Omar Valencia-Mendez 2017 harlequin shrimp length paper
G. Curt Fiedler 2002 harlequin shrimp sea star feeding paper
Omar Valencia-Mendez harlequin shrimp total length measurement
G Curt Fiedler 2002 sea star size harlequin shrimp experiment
working_plan: Search for Omar Valencia-Mendez 2017 paper on harlequin shrimp and find total length measurement
Search for G. Curt Fiedler 2002 paper on harlequin shrimp feeding and find sea star size
Extract the numerical values from both papers
Calculate percentage: (sea star size / harlequin shrimp length) * 100
Verify the calculation and source accuracy

solver.reasoning_summary: Found both required papers: Omar Valencia-Mendez 2017 recorded harlequin shrimp total length as ~4.5cm, and G. Curt Fiedler 2002 fed sea star pieces of approximately 1cm to the same species. Calculated percentage: (1cm / 4.5cm) × 100 = 22.222%, which rounds to 22.

artifacts_used.excerpt:
THE INFLUENCE OF SOCIAL ENVIRONMENT
ON SEX DETERMINATION IN HARLEQUIN SHRIMP
(HYMENOCERA PICTA : DECAPODA, GNATHOPHYLLIDAE)
G. Curt Fiedler
Sesoko Station, Tropical Biosphere Research Center, University of the Ryukyus, 3422 Sesoko,
Motobu-cho, Okinawa 905-0227, Japan (e-mail: kaatosan@hotmail.com)
ABSTRACT
Harlequin shrimp, Hymenocera picta , are monogamous and pair-bonding, and are usually found in
isolated singles and pairs in the ﬁeld. The apparent rarity of this species in their habitat and high levels of
aggression between consexuals suggests the possibility of some sort of plasticity in

Annotator Metadata.Steps:
1. Searched "Omar Valencfia-Mendez 2017 shrimp paper" on Google.
2. Opened "Decapoda: Palaemonidae: Hymenocera picta Dana, 1852) ..." on https://www.threatenedtaxa.org/index.php/JoTT/article/view/3238.
3. Clicked "PDF/A".
4. Found the length of the recorded shrimp as TL in the paper (4.5cm).
5. Searched "G. Curt Fiedler 2002 shrimp paper" on Google.
6. Opened "(PDF) The influence of social environment on sex ..." on https://www.researchgate.net/publication/232696279_The_influence_of_social_environment_on_sex_determination_in_harlequin_shrimp_Hymenocera_picta_Decapoda_Gnathophyllidae.
7. Found the size of the sea star fed to the shrimp (1cm).
8. Took the percentage (1 / 4.5 * 100% = 22.22222%).
9. Rounded to the nearest integer (22%).
Annotator Metadata.Tools:
1. Web browser
2. Search engine
3. PDF access
4. Calculator
